21	class TopKTopPSampler(nn.Module):
22	    """
23	    Module that performs optional top-k and top-p filtering followed by
24	    weighted random sampling of logits.
25	
26	    Implementations may update the logits tensor in-place.
27	    """
28	
29	    def __init__(self):
30	        super().__init__()
31	        if current_platform.is_cuda():
32	            if is_flashinfer_available:
33	                flashinfer_version = flashinfer.__version__
34	                if flashinfer_version < "0.2.3":
35	                    logger.warning(
36	                        "FlashInfer version >= 0.2.3 required. "
37	                        "Falling back to default sampling implementation.")
38	                    self.forward = self.forward_native
39	                elif envs.VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER is not False:
40	                    # NOTE(woosuk): The V0 sampler doesn't use FlashInfer for
41	                    # sampling unless VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER=1 (i.e., by
42	                    # default it is unused). For backward compatibility, we set
43	                    # `VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER` as None by default and
44	                    # interpret it differently in V0 and V1 samplers: In V0,
45	                    # None means False, while in V1, None means True. This is
46	                    # why we use the condition
47	                    # `envs.VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER is not False` here.
48	                    logger.info("Using FlashInfer for top-p & top-k sampling.")
49	                    self.forward = self.forward_cuda
50	                else:
51	                    logger.warning(
52	                        "FlashInfer is available, but it is not enabled. "
53	                        "Falling back to the PyTorch-native implementation of "
54	                        "top-p & top-k sampling. For the best performance, "
55	                        "please set VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER=1.")
56	                    self.forward = self.forward_native
57	            else:
58	                logger.warning(
59	                    "FlashInfer is not available. Falling back to the PyTorch-"
60	                    "native implementation of top-p & top-k sampling. For the "
61	                    "best performance, please install FlashInfer.")
62	                self.forward = self.forward_native
63	        elif current_platform.is_tpu():
64	            self.forward = self.forward_tpu
65	        else:
66	            self.forward = self.forward_native
67	
68	    def forward_native(
69	        self,
70	        logits: torch.Tensor,
71	        generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
72	        k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
73	        p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
74	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
75	        """
76	        PyTorch-native implementation of top-k and top-p sampling.
77	
78	        The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
79	        """
80	        logits = apply_top_k_top_p(logits, k, p)
81	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
82	        return random_sample(probs, generators)
83	
84	    def forward_cuda(
85	        self,
86	        logits: torch.Tensor,
87	        generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
88	        k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
89	        p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
90	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
91	        """More optimized implementation for top-k and top-p sampling."""
92	        if k is None and p is None:
93	            # We prefer `random_sample` over `flashinfer_sample` when sorting is
94	            # not needed. This is because `random_sample` does not require
95	            # CPU-GPU synchronization while `flashinfer_sample` does.
96	            probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
97	            return random_sample(probs, generators)
98	        if generators:
99	            logger.warning("FlashInfer 0.2.3+ does not support "
100	                           "per-request generators. Falling back to "
101	                           "PyTorch-native implementation.")
102	            return self.forward_native(logits, generators, k, p)
103	        # flashinfer sampling functions expect contiguous logits.
104	        # In flex_attn/triton_attn fp32 inference, logits can be non-contiguous
105	        # because of slicing operation in logits_processor.
106	        return flashinfer_sample(logits.contiguous(), k, p, generators)
107	
108	    def forward_tpu(
109	        self,
110	        logits: torch.Tensor,
111	        generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
112	        k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
113	        p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
114	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
115	        logits = apply_top_k_top_p_tpu(logits, k, p)
116	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
117	        return random_sample(probs, generators)
118	
119	
120	def apply_top_k_top_p_tpu(
121	    logits: torch.Tensor,
122	    k: torch.Tensor,
123	    p: torch.Tensor,
124	) -> torch.Tensor:
125	    """
126	    Apply top-k and top-p optimized for TPU.
127	
128	    This algorithm avoids using torch.scatter which is extremely slow on TPU.
129	    This is achieved by finding a "cut-off" element in the original logit, and
130	    after thresholding the logit using this cut-off, the remaining elements
131	    shall constitute the top-p set.
132	
133	    Note: in the case of tie (i.e. multipple cut-off elements present in the
134	    logit), all tie elements are included in the top-p set. In other words,
135	    this function does not break ties. Instead, these tie tokens have equal
136	    chance of being chosen during final sampling, so we can consider the tie
137	    being broken then.
138	    """
139	    probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1)
140	    probs_sort, _ = probs.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
141	
142	    if k is not None:
143	        top_k_count = probs_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)  # shape: (batch, )
144	        top_k_count = top_k_count.unsqueeze(dim=1)
145	        top_k_cutoff = probs_sort.gather(-1, top_k_count)
146	
147	        # Make sure the no top-k rows are no-op.
148	        no_top_k_mask = (k == logits.shape[1]).unsqueeze(dim=1)
149	        top_k_cutoff.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
150	
151	        elements_to_discard = probs < top_k_cutoff
152	        logits.masked_fill_(elements_to_discard, -float("inf"))
153	
154	    if p is not None:
155	        cumprob = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1)
156	        top_p_mask = cumprob <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
157	        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False  # at least one
158	
159	        top_p_count = top_p_mask.sum(dim=-1).unsqueeze(1)
160	        top_p_cutoff = probs_sort.gather(-1, top_p_count)
161	        elements_to_discard = probs < top_p_cutoff
162	        logits.masked_fill_(elements_to_discard, -float("inf"))
163	
164	    return logits
165	
166	
167	def apply_top_k_top_p(
168	    logits: torch.Tensor,
169	    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
170	    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
171	) -> torch.Tensor:
172	    """Apply top-k and top-p masks to the logits.
173	
174	    If a top-p is used, this function will sort the logits tensor,
175	    which can be slow for large batches.
176	
177	    The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
178	    """
179	    if p is None:
180	        if k is None:
181	            return logits
182	
183	        # Avoid sorting vocab for top-k only case.
184	        return apply_top_k_only(logits, k)
185	
186	    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
187	
188	    if k is not None:
189	        # Apply top-k.
190	        top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)  # shape: B
191	        # Get all the top_k values.
192	        top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
193	        top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
194	        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
195	
196	    if p is not None:
197	        # Apply top-p.
198	        probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
199	        probs_sum = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1, out=probs_sort)
200	        top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
201	        # at least one
202	        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
203	        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
204	
205	    # Re-sort the probabilities.
206	    logits = logits_sort.scatter(dim=-1, index=logits_idx, src=logits_sort)
207	    return logits
208	
209	
210	def apply_top_k_only(
211	    logits: torch.Tensor,
212	    k: torch.Tensor,
213	) -> torch.Tensor:
214	    """
215	    Apply top-k mask to the logits.
216	
217	    This implementation doesn't involve sorting the entire vocab.
218	
219	    The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
220	    """
221	    no_top_k_mask = k == logits.shape[1]
222	    # Set non-top-k rows to 1 so that we can gather.
223	    k = k.masked_fill(no_top_k_mask, 1)
224	    max_top_k = k.max()
225	    # topk.values tensor has shape [batch_size, max_top_k].
226	    # Convert top k to 0-based index in range [0, max_top_k).
227	    k_index = k.sub_(1).unsqueeze(1)
228	    top_k_mask = logits.topk(max_top_k, dim=1).values.gather(1, k_index.long())
229	    # Handle non-topk rows.
230	    top_k_mask.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask.unsqueeze(1), -float("inf"))
231	    logits.masked_fill_(logits < top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
232	    return logits
233	
234	
235	def random_sample(
236	    probs: torch.Tensor,
237	    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
238	) -> torch.Tensor:
239	    """Randomly sample from the probabilities.
240	
241	    We use this function instead of torch.multinomial because torch.multinomial
242	    causes CPU-GPU synchronization.
243	    """
244	    q = torch.empty_like(probs)
245	    # NOTE(woosuk): To batch-process the requests without their own seeds,
246	    # which is the common case, we first assume that every request does
247	    # not have its own seed. Then, we overwrite the values for the requests
248	    # that have their own seeds.
249	    if len(generators) != probs.shape[0]:
250	        q.exponential_()
251	    if generators:
252	        # TODO(woosuk): This can be slow because we handle each request
253	        # one by one. Optimize this.
254	        for i, generator in generators.items():
255	            q[i].exponential_(generator=generator)
256	    return probs.div_(q).argmax(dim=-1).view(-1)
257	
258	
259	def flashinfer_sample(
260	    logits: torch.Tensor,
261	    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
262	    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
263	    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
264	) -> torch.Tensor:
265	    """Sample from the logits using FlashInfer.
266	
267	    Statistically, this function is equivalent to the `random_sample` function.
268	    However, this function is faster because it avoids sorting the logits tensor
269	    via rejection sampling.
270	
271	    NOTE: The outputs of this function do not necessarily match the outputs of
272	    the `random_sample` function. It only guarantees that the outputs are
273	    statistically equivalent.
274	
275	    NOTE: This function includes CPU-GPU synchronization, while `random_sample`
276	    does not. Call this function at the end of the forward pass to minimize
277	    the synchronization overhead.
278	    """
279	    assert not (k is None and p is None)
280	    if k is None:
281	        # Top-p only.
282	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
283	        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_p_sampling_from_probs(
284	            probs, p, deterministic=True)
285	    elif p is None:
286	        # Top-k only.
287	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
288	        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_k_sampling_from_probs(
289	            probs, k, deterministic=True)
290	    else:
291	        # Both top-k and top-p.
292	        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_k_top_p_sampling_from_logits(
293	            logits, k, p, deterministic=True)
294	
295	    return next_token_ids.view(-1)

3	"""A layer that samples the next tokens from the model's outputs."""
4	
5	import torch
6	import torch.nn as nn
7	
8	from vllm.utils import is_pin_memory_available

10	from vllm.v1.sample.metadata import SamplingMetadata
11	from vllm.v1.sample.ops.bad_words import apply_bad_words
12	from vllm.v1.sample.ops.penalties import apply_all_penalties
13	from vllm.v1.sample.ops.topk_topp_sampler import TopKTopPSampler
14	
15	_SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-5
16	
17	
18	class Sampler(nn.Module):
19	
20	    def __init__(self):
21	        super().__init__()
22	        self.topk_topp_sampler = TopKTopPSampler()
23	        self.pin_memory = is_pin_memory_available()
24	
25	    def forward(
26	        self,
27	        logits: torch.Tensor,
28	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
29	    ) -> SamplerOutput:
30	        # NOTE(woosuk): Use the original logits (before any penalties or
31	        # temperature scaling) for the top-k logprobs.
32	        # This is different from the V0 sampler, which uses the logits that
33	        # is used for sampling (after penalties and temperature scaling).
34	        # TODO(rob): provide option for logprobs post sampling.
35	        # See https://vllm-dev.slack.com/archives/C07UUL8E61Z/p1735907856007919 # noqa: E501
36	        num_logprobs = sampling_metadata.max_num_logprobs
37	        if num_logprobs is not None:
38	            raw_logprobs = self.compute_logprobs(logits)
39	
40	        # Use float32 for the logits.
41	        logits = logits.to(torch.float32)
42	        # Apply allowed token ids.
43	        logits = self.apply_allowed_token_ids(logits, sampling_metadata)
44	        # Apply bad words exclusion.
45	        logits = self.apply_bad_words(logits, sampling_metadata)
46	
47	        # Apply logits processors which can impact greedy sampling
48	        for processor in (sampling_metadata.logitsprocs.non_argmax_invariant):
49	            logits = processor.apply(logits)
50	
51	        # Apply penalties (e.g., min_tokens, freq_penalties).
52	        logits = self.apply_penalties(logits, sampling_metadata)
53	        # Sample the next token.
54	        sampled = self.sample(logits, sampling_metadata)
55	        # Convert sampled token ids to int64 (long) type to ensure compatibility
56	        # with subsequent operations that may use these values as indices.
57	        # This conversion is necessary because FlashInfer sampling operations
58	        # return int32 (while PyTorch argmax and topk return int64).
59	        sampled = sampled.long()
60	
61	        # Gather the logprobs of the topk and sampled token (if requested).
62	        # Get logprobs and rank tensors (if requested)
63	        logprobs_tensors = None if num_logprobs is None else \
64	            self.gather_logprobs(raw_logprobs, num_logprobs, token_ids=sampled)
65	
66	        # Use int32 to reduce the tensor size.
67	        sampled = sampled.to(torch.int32)
68	
69	        # These are GPU tensors.
70	        sampler_output = SamplerOutput(
71	            # The sampled tokens are expanded to 2D tensor with shape
72	            # [num_requests, 1], where each row represents one generated
73	            # token per request.
74	            sampled_token_ids=sampled.unsqueeze(-1),
75	            logprobs_tensors=logprobs_tensors,
76	        )
77	        return sampler_output
78	
79	    def apply_temperature(
80	        self,
81	        logits: torch.Tensor,
82	        temp: torch.Tensor,
83	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
84	        # Use in-place division to avoid creating a new tensor.
85	        return logits.div_(temp.unsqueeze(dim=1))
86	
87	    def greedy_sample(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
88	        return logits.argmax(dim=-1).view(-1)
89	
90	    def sample(
91	        self,
92	        logits: torch.Tensor,
93	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
94	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
95	        """Sample logits based on sampling metadata.
96	
97	        The various logits processing functions called in this method
98	        may update the logits tensor in-place.
99	        """
100	
101	        assert not (sampling_metadata.all_greedy
102	                    and sampling_metadata.all_random)
103	        if sampling_metadata.all_random:
104	            greedy_sampled = None
105	        else:
106	            greedy_sampled = self.greedy_sample(logits)
107	            if sampling_metadata.all_greedy:
108	                return greedy_sampled
109	
110	        assert sampling_metadata.temperature is not None
111	
112	        # Apply temperature.
113	        logits = self.apply_temperature(logits, sampling_metadata.temperature)
114	
115	        # Apply logits processors that only apply to random sampling
116	        # (argmax invariant)
117	        for processor in sampling_metadata.logitsprocs.argmax_invariant:
118	            logits = processor.apply(logits)
119	
120	        # Apply top_k and/or top_p.
121	        random_sampled = self.topk_topp_sampler(
122	            logits,
123	            sampling_metadata.generators,
124	            sampling_metadata.top_k,
125	            sampling_metadata.top_p,
126	        )
127	
128	        if greedy_sampled is None:
129	            return random_sampled
130	
131	        sampled = torch.where(
132	            sampling_metadata.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS,
133	            greedy_sampled,
134	            random_sampled,
135	            out=greedy_sampled,  # Reuse tensor
136	        )
137	        return sampled
138	
139	    def compute_logprobs(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
140	        return logits.log_softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
141	
142	    def gather_logprobs(
143	        self,
144	        logprobs: torch.Tensor,
145	        num_logprobs: int,
146	        token_ids: torch.Tensor,
147	    ) -> LogprobsTensors:
148	        """
149	        Gather logprobs for topk and sampled/prompt token.
150	
151	        Args:
152	          logprobs: (num tokens) x (vocab) tensor
153	          num_logprobs: minimum number of logprobs to
154	                        retain per token
155	          token_ids: prompt tokens (if prompt logprobs)
156	                     or sampled tokens (if sampled
157	                     logprobs); 1D token ID tensor
158	                     with (num tokens) elements
159	                     Must be int64.
160	
161	        Returns:
162	          Top-k int indices tensor, (num tokens) x (num_logprobs + 1)
163	          Top-k float logprobs tensor, (num tokens) x (num_logprobs + 1)
164	          Sampled token rank tensor, (num tokens)
165	        """
166	        assert token_ids.dtype == torch.int64
167	        # Find the topK values.
168	        topk_logprobs, topk_indices = torch.topk(logprobs,
169	                                                 num_logprobs,
170	                                                 dim=-1)
171	
172	        # Get with the logprob of the prompt or sampled token.
173	        token_ids = token_ids.unsqueeze(-1)
174	        token_logprobs = logprobs.gather(-1, token_ids)
175	
176	        # Compute the ranks of the actual token.
177	        token_ranks = (logprobs >= token_logprobs).sum(-1)
178	
179	        # Concatenate together with the topk.
180	        indices = torch.cat((token_ids, topk_indices), dim=1)
181	        logprobs = torch.cat((token_logprobs, topk_logprobs), dim=1)
182	
183	        # Use int32 to reduce the tensor size.
184	        indices = indices.to(torch.int32)
185	
186	        return LogprobsTensors(indices, logprobs, token_ranks)
187	
188	    def apply_penalties(
189	        self,
190	        logits: torch.Tensor,
191	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
192	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
193	        if not sampling_metadata.no_penalties:
194	            assert sampling_metadata.prompt_token_ids is not None
195	            logits = apply_all_penalties(
196	                logits,
197	                sampling_metadata.prompt_token_ids,
198	                sampling_metadata.presence_penalties,
199	                sampling_metadata.frequency_penalties,
200	                sampling_metadata.repetition_penalties,

Excellent! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me compile all the findings into a detailed report.

## Summary: top_p and top_k Sampling Parameters in vLLM

Based on my thorough exploration of the vLLM codebase, here's a detailed breakdown of how top_p and top_k sampling parameters are implemented:

### 1. **SamplingParams Class Definition**
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331308236-uex7al/vllm/sampling_params.py`

#### Parameter Definitions (lines 212-213):
```python
top_p: float = 1.0
top_k: int = 0
```

#### Docstring Documentation (lines 150-153):
- **top_p**: "Float that controls the cumulative probability of the top tokens to consider. Must be in (0, 1]. Set to 1 to consider all tokens."
- **top_k**: "Integer that controls the number of top tokens to consider. Set to 0 (or -1) to consider all tokens."

#### Validation Logic (lines 417-425):
```python
if not 0.0 < self.top_p <= 1.0:
    raise ValueError(f"top_p must be in (0, 1], got {self.top_p}.")
# quietly accept -1 as disabled, but prefer 0
if self.top_k < -1:
    raise ValueError(f"top_k must be 0 (disable), or at least 1, "
                     f"got {self.top_k}.")
if not isinstance(self.top_k, int):
    raise TypeError(
        f"top_k must be an integer, got {type(self.top_k).__name__}")
```

#### Greedy Sampling Override (lines 377-382):
When temperature approaches 0 (greedy sampling), both parameters are reset:
```python
if self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
    # Zero temperature means greedy sampling.
    self.top_p = 1.0
    self.top_k = 0
    self.min_p = 0.0
    self._verify_greedy_sampling()
```

### 2. **Intermediate Data Structures**

#### A. **SequenceGroupMetadata**

This carries `sampling_params: Optional[SamplingParams]` as a field, passing the full SamplingParams object downstream:

### 4. **Top-K and Top-P Implementation**

**Algorithm** (`_apply_top_k_top_p()` in sampler.py, lines 398-424):
```python
def _apply_top_k_top_p(logits, p, k):
    # Sort logits
    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
    
    # Apply top-k: mask out all except top-k tokens
    top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
    top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
    top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
    
    # Apply top-p: mask out tokens with cumulative probability > (1-p)
    probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
    probs_sum = probs_sort.cumsum(dim=-1)
    top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
    top_p_mask[:, -1] = False  # Keep at least one token
    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
    
    # Re-sort back to original logits order
    logits = torch.empty_like(logits_sort).scatter_(dim=-1,
                                                    index=logits_idx,
                                                    src=logits_sort)
    return logits
```

```python
)
```

### 6. **Key Processing Details**

- **Top-K Clamping**: top_k is clamped to vocabulary size (line 419 in sampling_metadata.py)
- **Default Handling**: If top_k < 1, it defaults to vocab_size (no filtering), line 420
- **Optimization Flags**: `do_top_p_top_k` boolean computed to skip top-p/top-k processing when both are at defaults (lines 426-428)
- **Pin Memory**: All tensors use pinned memory for non-blocking GPU transfers (lines 543-580)
- **Per-Sequence Parameters**: Each sequence group can have different top_p/top_k values within the same batch

| File | Purpose | Key Lines |
|------|---------|-----------|
| `vllm/sampling_params.py` | Parameter definition, validation, docstrings | 118-589 |
| `vllm/sequence.py` | SequenceGroupMetadata carrying SamplingParams | 990-1087 |
| `vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` | Tensor extraction and batch processing | 97-597 |
| `vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` | Actual sampling algorithm | 225-424 |

- Accept flexible sampling parameters per request
- Validate parameters early in the pipeline
- Batch process samples with per-sequence parameters
- Efficiently apply filtering on GPU with pinned memory transfers
- Support both standard and optimized (FlashInfer) implementations

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331308236-uex7al/vllm/sampling_params.py`

- **top_p**: "Float that controls the cumulative probability of the top tokens to consider. Must be in (0, 1]. Set to 1 to consider all tokens."
- **top_k**: "Integer that controls the number of top tokens to consider. Set to 0 (or -1) to consider all tokens."

#### Validation Logic (lines 417-425):
```python
if not 0.0 < self.top_p <= 1.0:
    raise ValueError(f"top_p must be in (0, 1], got {self.top_p}.")
# quietly accept -1 as disabled, but prefer 0
if self.top_k < -1:
    raise ValueError(f"top_k must be 0 (disable), or at least 1, "
                     f"got {self.top_k}.")

    raise TypeError(
        f"top_k must be an integer, got {type(self.top_k).__name__}")
```

#### Greedy Sampling Override (lines 377-382):
When temperature approaches 0 (greedy sampling), both parameters are reset:
```python
if self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
```

Excellent! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a detailed exploration report. Since this is in plan mode, I'll organize my findings into a thorough analysis document.

Based on my thorough exploration of the vLLM codebase, here's a comprehensive analysis of the top_p/top_k filtering logic and where to implement Top-A sampling:

## Exploration Summary: vLLM Top-P/Top-K Filtering & Top-A Implementation Plan

### Current Implementation Overview

vLLM has **two main sampling architectures**:
1. **V0 (Legacy)**: `/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py`
2. **V1 (New)**: `/vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py` with ops in `/vllm/v1/sample/ops/`

---

## Key Files Found

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `/vllm/sampling_params.py` | Defines `SamplingParams` dataclass with top_p, top_k, min_p parameters |
| `/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` | V0 sampler - main sampling logic |
| `/vllm/model_executor/layers/logits_processor.py` | Logits processing layer |
| `/vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` | `SamplingTensors` struct that batches sampling parameters |
| `/vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py` | V1 sampler (newer architecture) |
| `/vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py` | V1 top-k/top-p filtering implementation |

---

## V0 Sampler: Top-P/Top-K Filtering Logic

**Location**: `/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py:397-423`

### `_apply_top_k_top_p()` Function

This is the core V0 filtering function. Here's the exact logic:

```python
def _apply_top_k_top_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,  # shape: (num_tokens, vocab_size)
    p: torch.Tensor,       # shape: (num_tokens,) - top_p values per sequence
    k: torch.Tensor,       # shape: (num_tokens,) - top_k values per sequence
) -> torch.Tensor:
    # 1. Sort logits in ascending order
    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)

    # 2. Apply top-k: mask out all tokens below the k-th highest logit
    top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)  # vocab_size - k
    top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
    top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask  # tokens below top-k threshold
    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))

    # 3. Apply top-p: mask out cumulative probability tail
    probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
    probs_sum = probs_sort.cumsum(dim=-1)  # cumulative probability
    top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)  # tokens in tail
    top_p_mask[:, -1] = False  # ALWAYS keep at least one token
    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))

    # 4. Re-scatter back to original order
    logits = torch.empty_like(logits_sort).scatter_(dim=-1, index=logits_idx, src=logits_sort)
    return logits
```

### Also See: `_apply_min_p()` (lines 426-440)

This is similar to your Top-A proposal! It does exactly what you want:

```python
def _apply_min_p(logits: torch.Tensor, min_p: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)  # max prob per sequence
    scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs  # threshold = min_p * max_p
    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_min_p  # mask tokens below threshold
    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
    return logits
```

---

## V1 Sampler: Top-K/Top-P Implementation

**Location**: `/vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py`

V1 has more optimized implementations:

### `apply_top_k_top_p()` (lines 167-207)
- Same logic as V0 but uses `scatter()` instead of `empty_like().scatter_()`
- Supports both PyTorch-native and FlashInfer backends

### `apply_top_k_only()` (lines 210-232)
- Optimized for top-k-only case (avoids full sort)
- Uses `topk()` instead of `sort()`

### `apply_top_k_top_p_tpu()` (lines 120-164)
- TPU-optimized version that avoids slow `scatter()`

---

## Data Flow: How Sampling Parameters Get to Filtering

### 1. **SamplingParams Definition** (`sampling_params.py:118-245`)
```python
class SamplingParams:
    top_p: float = 1.0      # default: no filtering
    top_k: int = 0          # default: 0 = disabled
    min_p: float = 0.0      # default: disabled
```

### 2. **SamplingTensors Creation** (`sampling_metadata.py:371-596`)
The `SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata()` class method batches all parameters:
- Extracts `top_p`, `top_k`, `min_p` from each sequence group's `SamplingParams`
- Creates tensors: `top_ps`, `top_ks`, `min_ps` (shape: `[num_sequences]`)
- These tensors are created with pinned memory for fast GPU transfer

**Key code** (lines 386-494):
```python
@classmethod
def from_sampling_metadata(...) -> tuple["SamplingTensors", bool, bool, bool]:
    # ... build lists from seq_groups ...
    for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
        sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
        top_p = sampling_params.top_p
        top_k = min(sampling_params.top_k, vocab_size)
        min_p = sampling_params.min_p
        # ... append to top_ps, top_ks, min_ps lists ...
    
    # Convert lists to GPU tensors
    return SamplingTensors(
        top_ps=top_ps_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
        top_ks=top_ks_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
        min_ps=min_ps_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
        ...
    )
```

### 3. **V0 Sampler Forward Pass** (`sampler.py:224-305`)
```python
def forward(self, logits: torch.Tensor, sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata):
    # Initialize sampling tensors
    sampling_tensors = SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata(...)
    
    # Apply top-p/top-k filtering
    if do_top_p_top_k and flashinfer_top_k_top_p_sampling is None:
        logits = _apply_top_k_top_p(logits, sampling_tensors.top_ps,
                                    sampling_tensors.top_ks)
    
    # Apply min_p filtering
    if do_min_p:
        logits = _apply_min_p(logits, sampling_tensors.min_ps)
    
    # Compute probabilities and sample
    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1, dtype=torch.float)
    maybe_deferred_sample_results, _ = _sample(probs, logprobs, ...)
```

---

## Where to Add Top-A Sampling

### **Option 1: V0 Sampler (Recommended for broader compatibility)**

**Files to modify:**

1. **`/vllm/sampling_params.py`** (~lines 150-160)
   - Add new field: `top_a: float = 0.0` to `SamplingParams`
   - Add validation in `_verify_args()` (~line 387): `if not 0.0 <= self.top_a <= 1.0`

2. **`/vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py`** (~lines 371-596)
   - Add `top_as: torch.Tensor` field to `SamplingTensors` dataclass (line 378, after `top_ks`)
   - In `from_sampling_metadata()`, extract `sampling_params.top_a` values (line 415)
   - Build `top_as` tensor list (line 397)
   - Add `top_as` to `from_lists()` call (line 480)

Based on my thorough exploration of the vLLM codebase, here's a comprehensive analysis of the top_p/top_k filtering logic and where to implement Top-A sampling:

## Exploration Summary: vLLM Top-P/Top-K Filtering & Top-A Implementation Plan

### Current Implementation Overview

vLLM has **two main sampling architectures**:
1. **V0 (Legacy)**: `/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py`
2. **V1 (New)**: `/vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py` with ops in `/vllm/v1/sample/ops/`

---

## Key Files Found

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `/vllm/sampling_params.py` | Defines `SamplingParams` dataclass with top_p, top_k, min_p parameters |
| `/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` | V0 sampler - main sampling logic |
| `/vllm/model_executor/layers/logits_processor.py` | Logits processing layer |
| `/vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` | `SamplingTensors` struct that batches sampling parameters |
| `/vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py` | V1 sampler (newer architecture) |
| `/vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py` | V1 top-k/top-p filtering implementation |

---

## V0 Sampler: Top-P/Top-K Filtering Logic

**Location**: `/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py:397-423`

### `_apply_top_k_top_p()` Function

This is the core V0 filtering function. Here's the exact logic:

```python
def _apply_top_k_top_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,  # shape: (num_tokens, vocab_size)
    p: torch.Tensor,       # shape: (num_tokens,) - top_p values per sequence
    k: torch.Tensor,       # shape: (num_tokens,) - top_k values per sequence
) -> torch.Tensor:
    # 1. Sort logits in ascending order
    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)

    # 2. Apply top-k: mask out all tokens below the k-th highest logit
    top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)  # vocab_size - k
    top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
    top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask  # tokens below top-k threshold
    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))

    # 3. Apply top-p: mask out cumulative probability tail
    probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
    probs_sum = probs_sort.cumsum(dim=-1)  # cumulative probability
    top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)  # tokens in tail
    top_p_mask[:, -1] = False  # ALWAYS keep at least one token
    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))

    # 4. Re-scatter back to original order
    logits = torch.empty_like(logits_sort).scatter_(dim=-1, index=logits_idx, src=logits_sort)
    return logits
```

### Also See: `_apply_min_p()` (lines 426-440)

This is similar to your Top-A proposal! It does exactly what you want:

```python
def _apply_min_p(logits: torch.Tensor, min_p: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)  # max prob per sequence
    scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs  # threshold = min_p * max_p
    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_min_p  # mask tokens below threshold
    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
    return logits
```

---

## V1 Sampler: Top-K/Top-P Implementation

**Location**: `/vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py`

V1 has more optimized implementations:

### `apply_top_k_top_p()` (lines 167-207)
- Same logic as V0 but uses `scatter()` instead of `empty_like().scatter_()`
- Supports both PyTorch-native and FlashInfer backends

### `apply_top_k_only()` (lines 210-232)
- Optimized for top-k-only case (avoids full sort)
- Uses `topk()` instead of `sort()`

### `apply_top_k_top_p_tpu()` (lines 120-164)
- TPU-optimized version that avoids slow `scatter()`

---

## Data Flow: How Sampling Parameters Get to Filtering

### 1. **SamplingParams Definition** (`sampling_params.py:118-245`)
```python
class SamplingParams:
    top_p: float = 1.0      # default: no filtering
    top_k: int = 0          # default: 0 = disabled
    min_p: float = 0.0      # default: disabled
```

### 2. **SamplingTensors Creation** (`sampling_metadata.py:371-596`)
The `SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata()` class method batches all parameters:
- Extracts `top_p`, `top_k`, `min_p` from each sequence group's `SamplingParams`
- Creates tensors: `top_ps`, `top_ks`, `min_ps` (shape: `[num_sequences]`)
- These tensors are created with pinned memory for fast GPU transfer

**Key code** (lines 386-494):
```python
@classmethod
def from_sampling_metadata(...) -> tuple["SamplingTensors", bool, bool, bool]:
    # ... build lists from seq_groups ...
    for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
        sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
        top_p = sampling_params.top_p
        top_k = min(sampling_params.top_k, vocab_size)
        min_p = sampling_params.min_p
        # ... append to top_ps, top_ks, min_ps lists ...
    
    # Convert lists to GPU tensors
    return SamplingTensors(
        top_ps=top_ps_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
        top_ks=top_ks_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
        min_ps=min_ps_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
        ...
    )
```

### 3. **V0 Sampler Forward Pass** (`sampler.py:224-305`)
```python
def forward(self, logits: torch.Tensor, sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata):
    # Initialize sampling tensors
    sampling_tensors = SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata(...)
    
    # Apply top-p/top-k filtering
    if do_top_p_top_k and flashinfer_top_k_top_p_sampling is None:
        logits = _apply_top_k_top_p(logits, sampling_tensors.top_ps,
                                    sampling_tensors.top_ks)
    
    # Apply min_p filtering
    if do_min_p:
        logits = _apply_min_p(logits, sampling_tensors.min_ps)
    
    # Compute probabilities and sample
    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1, dtype=torch.float)
    maybe_deferred_sample_results, _ = _sample(probs, logprobs, ...)
```

---

110	    # Return entire output so far in every RequestOutput

122	        dict=True):  # type: ignore[call-arg]
123	    """Sampling parameters for text generation.
124	
125	    Overall, we follow the sampling parameters from the OpenAI text completion
126	    API (https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/completions/create).
127	    In addition, we support beam search, which is not supported by OpenAI.
128	
129	    Args:
130	        n: Number of output sequences to return for the given prompt.
131	        best_of: Number of output sequences that are generated from the prompt.
132	            From these `best_of` sequences, the top `n` sequences are returned.
133	            `best_of` must be greater than or equal to `n`. By default,
134	            `best_of` is set to `n`. Warning, this is only supported in V0.
135	        presence_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on whether they
136	            appear in the generated text so far. Values > 0 encourage the model
137	            to use new tokens, while values < 0 encourage the model to repeat
138	            tokens.
139	        frequency_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on their
140	            frequency in the generated text so far. Values > 0 encourage the
141	            model to use new tokens, while values < 0 encourage the model to
142	            repeat tokens.
143	        repetition_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on whether
144	            they appear in the prompt and the generated text so far. Values > 1
145	            encourage the model to use new tokens, while values < 1 encourage
146	            the model to repeat tokens.
147	        temperature: Float that controls the randomness of the sampling. Lower
148	            values make the model more deterministic, while higher values make
149	            the model more random. Zero means greedy sampling.
150	        top_p: Float that controls the cumulative probability of the top tokens
151	            to consider. Must be in (0, 1]. Set to 1 to consider all tokens.
152	        top_k: Integer that controls the number of top tokens to consider. Set
153	            to 0 (or -1) to consider all tokens.
154	        min_p: Float that represents the minimum probability for a token to be
155	            considered, relative to the probability of the most likely token.
156	            Must be in [0, 1]. Set to 0 to disable this.
157	        seed: Random seed to use for the generation.
158	        stop: list of strings that stop the generation when they are generated.
159	            The returned output will not contain the stop strings.
160	        stop_token_ids: list of tokens that stop the generation when they are

163	        bad_words: list of words that are not allowed to be generated.

178	            Note that the implementation follows the OpenAI API: The API will

# Return entire output so far in every RequestOutput
    CUMULATIVE = 0
    # Return only deltas in each RequestOutput
    DELTA = 1
    # Do not return intermediate RequestOutput
    FINAL_ONLY = 2


class SamplingParams(
        msgspec.Struct,
        omit_defaults=True,  # type: ignore[call-arg]
        # required for @cached_property.
        dict=True):  # type: ignore[call-arg]
    """Sampling parameters for text generation.

    Overall, we follow the sampling parameters from the OpenAI text completion
    API (https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/completions/create).
    In addition, we support beam search, which is not supported by OpenAI.

    Args:
        n: Number of output sequences to return for the given prompt.
        best_of: Number of output sequences that are generated from the prompt.
            From these `best_of` sequences, the top `n` sequences are returned.
            `best_of` must be greater than or equal to `n`. By default,
            `best_of` is set to `n`. Warning, this is only supported in V0.
        presence_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on whether they
            appear in the generated text so far. Values > 0 encourage the model
            to use new tokens, while values < 0 encourage the model to repeat
            tokens.
        frequency_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on their
            frequency in the generated text so far. Values > 0 encourage the
            model to use new tokens, while values < 0 encourage the model to
            repeat tokens.
        repetition_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on whether
            they appear in the prompt and the generated text so far. Values > 1
            encourage the model to use new tokens, while values < 1 encourage
            the model to repeat tokens.
        temperature: Float that controls the randomness of the sampling. Lower
            values make the model more deterministic, while higher values make
            the model more random. Zero means greedy sampling.
        top_p: Float that controls the cumulative probability of the top tokens
            to consider. Must be in (0, 1]. Set to 1 to consider all tokens.
        top_k: Integer that controls the number of top tokens to consider. Set
            to 0 (or -1) to consider all tokens.
        min_p: Float that represents the minimum probability for a token to be
            considered, relative to the probability of the most likely token.
            Must be in [0, 1]. Set to 0 to disable this.
        seed: Random seed to use for the generation.
        stop: list of strings that stop the generation when they are generated.
            The returned output will not contain the stop strings.
        stop_token_ids: list of tokens that stop the generation when they are
            generated. The returned output will contain the stop tokens unless
            the stop tokens are special tokens.
        bad_words: list of words that are not allowed to be generated.
            More precisely, only the last token of a corresponding
            token sequence is not allowed when the next generated token
            can complete the sequence.
        include_stop_str_in_output: Whether to include the stop strings in
            output text. Defaults to False.
        ignore_eos: Whether to ignore the EOS token and continue generating
            tokens after the EOS token is generated.
        max_tokens: Maximum number of tokens to generate per output sequence.
        min_tokens: Minimum number of tokens to generate per output sequence
            before EOS or stop_token_ids can be generated
        logprobs: Number of log probabilities to return per output token.
            When set to None, no probability is returned. If set to a non-None
            value, the result includes the log probabilities of the specified
            number of most likely tokens, as well as the chosen tokens.
            Note that the implementation follows the OpenAI API: The API will
            always return the log probability of the sampled token, so there
            may be up to `logprobs+1` elements in the response.
        prompt_logprobs: Number of log probabilities to return per prompt token.
        detokenize: Whether to detokenize the output. Defaults to True.
        skip_special_tokens: Whether to skip special tokens in the output.
        spaces_between_special_tokens: Whether to add spaces between special
            tokens in the output.  Defaults to True.
        logits_processors: list of functions that modify logits based on
            previously generated tokens, and optionally prompt tokens as
            a first argument.
        truncate_prompt_tokens: If set to -1, will use the truncation size
            supported by the model. If set to an integer k, will use only
            the last k tokens from the prompt (i.e., left truncation).
            Defaults to None (i.e., no truncation).
        guided_decoding: If provided, the engine will construct a guided
            decoding logits processor from these parameters. Defaults to None.
        logit_bias: If provided, the engine will construct a logits processor
            that applies these logit biases. Defaults to None.
        allowed_token_ids: If provided, the engine will construct a logits
            processor which only retains scores for the given token ids.
            Defaults to None.
        extra_args: Arbitrary additional args, that can be used by custom
            sampling implementations, plugins, etc. Not used by any in-tree
            sampling implementations.
    """

    n: int = 1
    best_of: Optional[int] = None
    _real_n: Optional[int] = None
    presence_penalty: float = 0.0
    frequency_penalty: float = 0.0
    repetition_penalty: float = 1.0
    temperature: float = 1.0
    top_p: float = 1.0
    top_k: int = 0
    min_p: float = 0.0
    seed: Optional[int] = None
    stop: Optional[Union[str, list[str]]] = None
    stop_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None
    ignore_eos: bool = False
    max_tokens: Optional[int] = 16
    min_tokens: int = 0
    logprobs: Optional[int] = None
    prompt_logprobs: Optional[int] = None
    # NOTE: This parameter is only exposed at the engine level for now.
    # It is not exposed in the OpenAI API server, as the OpenAI API does
    # not support returning only a list of token IDs.
    detokenize: bool = True
    skip_special_tokens: bool = True
    spaces_between_special_tokens: bool = True
    # Optional[list[LogitsProcessor]] type. We use Any here because
    # Optional[list[LogitsProcessor]] type is not supported by msgspec.
    logits_processors: Optional[Any] = None
    include_stop_str_in_output: bool = False
    truncate_prompt_tokens: Optional[Annotated[int, msgspec.Meta(ge=1)]] = None
    output_kind: RequestOutputKind = RequestOutputKind.CUMULATIVE

    # The below fields are not supposed to be used as an input.
    # They are set in post_init.
    output_text_buffer_length: int = 0
    _all_stop_token_ids: set[int] = msgspec.field(default_factory=set)

    # Fields used to construct logits processors
    guided_decoding: Optional[GuidedDecodingParams] = None
    logit_bias: Optional[dict[int, float]] = None
    allowed_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None
    extra_args: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None

    # Fields used for bad words
    bad_words: Optional[list[str]] = None
    _bad_words_token_ids: Optional[list[list[int]]] = None

    @staticmethod
    def from_optional(
        n: Optional[int] = 1,
        best_of: Optional[int] = None,
        presence_penalty: Optional[float] = 0.0,
        frequency_penalty: Optional[float] = 0.0,
        repetition_penalty: Optional[float] = 1.0,
        temperature: Optional[float] = 1.0,
        top_p: Optional[float] = 1.0,
        top_k: int = 0,
        min_p: float = 0.0,
        seed: Optional[int] = None,
        stop: Optional[Union[str, list[str]]] = None,
        stop_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None,
        bad_words: Optional[list[str]] = None,
        include_stop_str_in_output: bool = False,
        ignore_eos: bool = False,
        max_tokens: Optional[int] = 16,
        min_tokens: int = 0,
        logprobs: Optional[int] = None,
        prompt_logprobs: Optional[int] = None,
        detokenize: bool = True,
        skip_special_tokens: bool = True,
        spaces_between_special_tokens: bool = True,
        logits_processors: Optional[list[LogitsProcessor]] = None,
        truncate_prompt_tokens: Optional[Annotated[int,
                                                   msgspec.Meta(ge=1)]] = None,
        output_kind: RequestOutputKind = RequestOutputKind.CUMULATIVE,
        guided_decoding: Optional[GuidedDecodingParams] = None,
        logit_bias: Optional[Union[dict[int, float], dict[str, float]]] = None,
        allowed_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None,
        extra_args: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None,
    ) -> "SamplingParams":
        if logit_bias is not None:
            # Convert token_id to integer
            # Clamp the bias between -100 and 100 per OpenAI API spec
            logit_bias = {
                int(token): min(100.0, max(-100.0, bias))
                for token, bias in logit_bias.items()
            }

        return SamplingParams(
            n=1 if n is None else n,
            best_of=best_of,
            presence_penalty=0.0
            if presence_penalty is None else presence_penalty,
            frequency_penalty=0.0
            if frequency_penalty is None else frequency_penalty,
            repetition_penalty=1.0
            if repetition_penalty is None else repetition_penalty,
            temperature=1.0 if temperature is None else temperature,
            top_p=1.0 if top_p is None else top_p,
            top_k=top_k,
            min_p=min_p,
            seed=seed,
            stop=stop,
            stop_token_ids=stop_token_ids,
            bad_words=bad_words,
            include_stop_str_in_output=include_stop_str_in_output,
            ignore_eos=ignore_eos,
            max_tokens=max_tokens,
            min_tokens=min_tokens,
            logprobs=logprobs,
            prompt_logprobs=prompt_logprobs,
            detokenize=detokenize,
            skip_special_tokens=skip_special_tokens,
            spaces_between_special_tokens=spaces_between_special_tokens,
            logits_processors=logits_processors,
            truncate_prompt_tokens=truncate_prompt_tokens,
            output_kind=output_kind,
            guided_decoding=guided_decoding,
            logit_bias=logit_bias,
            allowed_token_ids=allowed_token_ids,
            extra_args=extra_args,
        )

    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
        # how we deal with `best_of``:
        # if `best_of`` is not set, we default to `n`;
        # if `best_of`` is set, we set `n`` to `best_of`,
        # and set `_real_n`` to the original `n`.
        # when we return the result, we will check
        # if we need to return `n` or `_real_n` results
        if self.best_of:
            if self.best_of < self.n:
                raise ValueError(
                    f"best_of must be greater than or equal to n, "
                    f"got n={self.n} and best_of={self.best_of}.")
            if not self._real_n:
                self._real_n = self.n
                self.n = self.best_of

        if 0 < self.temperature < _MAX_TEMP:
            logger.warning(
                "temperature %s is less than %s, which may cause numerical "
                "errors nan or inf in tensors. We have maxed it out to %s.",
                self.temperature, _MAX_TEMP, _MAX_TEMP)
            self.temperature = max(self.temperature, _MAX_TEMP)

        if self.seed == -1:
            self.seed = None

        if self.stop is None:
            self.stop = []
        elif isinstance(self.stop, str):
            self.stop = [self.stop]

        if self.stop_token_ids is None:
            self.stop_token_ids = []

        if self.bad_words is None:
            self.bad_words = []

        if self.logprobs is True:
            self.logprobs = 1

        if self.prompt_logprobs is True:
            self.prompt_logprobs = 1

        # Number of characters to hold back for stop string evaluation
        # until sequence is finished.
        if self.stop and not self.include_stop_str_in_output:
            self.output_text_buffer_length = max(len(s) for s in self.stop) - 1

        self._verify_args()

        if self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
            # Zero temperature means greedy sampling.
            self.top_p = 1.0
            self.top_k = 0
            self.min_p = 0.0
            self._verify_greedy_sampling()

        # eos_token_id is added to this by the engine
        self._all_stop_token_ids.update(self.stop_token_ids)

    def _verify_args(self) -> None:
        if not isinstance(self.n, int):
            raise ValueError(f"n must be an int, but is of "
                             f"type {type(self.n)}")
        if self.n < 1:
            raise ValueError(f"n must be at least 1, got {self.n}.")
        if self.best_of is not None:
            if not isinstance(self.best_of, int):
                raise ValueError(
                    f"best_of must be an integer, got {type(self.best_of)}")
            if self.best_of < 1:
                raise ValueError(
                    f"best_of must be at least 1, got {self.best_of}")
            if self.best_of < self.n:
                raise ValueError(
                    f"best_of must be greater than or equal to n, "
                    f"got n={self.n} and best_of={self.best_of}.")
        if not -2.0 <= self.presence_penalty <= 2.0:
            raise ValueError("presence_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got "
                             f"{self.presence_penalty}.")
        if not -2.0 <= self.frequency_penalty <= 2.0:
            raise ValueError("frequency_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got "
                             f"{self.frequency_penalty}.")
        if self.repetition_penalty <= 0.0:
            raise ValueError(
                "repetition_penalty must be greater than zero, got "
                f"{self.repetition_penalty}.")
        if self.temperature < 0.0:
            raise ValueError(
                f"temperature must be non-negative, got {self.temperature}.")
        if not 0.0 < self.top_p <= 1.0:
            raise ValueError(f"top_p must be in (0, 1], got {self.top_p}.")
        # quietly accept -1 as disabled, but prefer 0
        if self.top_k < -1:
            raise ValueError(f"top_k must be 0 (disable), or at least 1, "
                             f"got {self.top_k}.")
        if not isinstance(self.top_k, int):
            raise TypeError(
                f"top_k must be an integer, got {type(self.top_k).__name__}")
        if not 0.0 <= self.min_p <= 1.0:
            raise ValueError("min_p must be in [0, 1], got "
                             f"{self.min_p}.")
        if self.max_tokens is not None and self.max_tokens < 1:

200	        self,
201	        logits: torch.Tensor,
202	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
203	    ):
204	        """The goal here is to reuse sampling tensors between similar decode
205	        runs. This is possible because sampling logic does not change between
206	        decodes of the same sequences.
207	        """
208	        _, vocab_size = logits.shape
209	
210	        # First free any existing stored sampling tensors.
211	        # This is necessary because some sampling tensors may
212	        # have pinned memory.
213	        self._sampling_tensors = None
214	
215	        # Initialize new sampling tensors
216	        (sampling_tensors, do_penalties, do_top_p_top_k,
217	         do_min_p) = SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata(
218	             sampling_metadata, vocab_size, logits.device, logits.dtype)
219	
220	        self._sampling_tensors = sampling_tensors
221	        self._do_penalties = do_penalties
222	        self._do_top_p_top_k = do_top_p_top_k
223	        self._do_min_p = do_min_p
224	
225	    def forward(
226	        self,
227	        logits: torch.Tensor,
228	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
229	    ) -> Optional[SamplerOutput]:
230	        """
231	        Single-step scheduling:
232	            * Perform GPU-side sampling computation & compute
233	            GPU-side logprobs tensor
234	            * Pythonize sampling result & logprobs tensor
235	
236	        Multi-step scheduling:
237	            * Perform GPU-side sampling computation & compute
238	            GPU-side logprobs tensor
239	            * Defer Pythonization of sampling result & logprobs
240	            tensor
241	            * Encapsulate arguments required for deferred Pythonization
242	            in the
243	            [`SamplerOutput`][vllm.model_executor.layers.sampler.SamplerOutput]
244	            structure
245	
246	        Args:
247	            logits: (num_tokens, vocab_size).
248	            sampling_metadata: Metadata for sampling.
249	        """
250	        assert logits is not None
251	        _, vocab_size = logits.shape
252	
253	        # Prepare sampling tensors with pinned memory to avoid blocking.
254	        if not sampling_metadata.reuse_sampling_tensors:
255	            self._init_sampling_tensors(logits, sampling_metadata)
256	        elif self._do_penalties:
257	            # In this case, the sampling tensors logic depends on
258	            # "output_tokens" of a sequence. As a result, we cannot
259	            # reuse sampling tensors, since "output_tokens" changes
260	            # between decode runs.
261	            self._init_sampling_tensors(logits, sampling_metadata)
262	
263	        assert self._sampling_tensors is not None
264	        sampling_tensors = self._sampling_tensors
265	        do_penalties = self._do_penalties
266	        do_top_p_top_k = self._do_top_p_top_k
267	        do_min_p = self._do_min_p
268

351	def _apply_min_tokens_penalty(
352	    logits: torch.Tensor,
353	    sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
354	) -> torch.Tensor:
355	    """Apply min_tokens penalty which sets stop tokens to -inf if min_tokens
356	        have not been generated yet
357	    """
358	    # list of indices in logits that will be set to -inf
359	    logits_to_penalize: list[tuple[int, int]] = []
360	    logits_applied = 0
361	    for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
362	        seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
363	        sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
364	
365	        sample_indices = seq_group.sample_indices
366	        logits_applied += len(sample_indices) + len(
367	            seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
368	        if not seq_group.do_sample:
369	            continue
370	
371	        start_idx = sample_indices[0]
372	        min_tokens = sampling_params.min_tokens
373	        token_ids_to_penalize = sampling_params.all_stop_token_ids
374	        if min_tokens > 0 and token_ids_to_penalize:
375	            seqs_to_penalize: list[int] = []
376	            for j, seq_id in enumerate(seq_ids):
377	                seq_data = seq_group.seq_data[seq_id]
378	                if len(seq_data.output_token_ids_array) < min_tokens:
379	                    seqs_to_penalize.append(j)
380	
381	            if seqs_to_penalize:
382	                # convert to the index into logits
383	                seqs_to_penalize = [start_idx + j for j in seqs_to_penalize]
384	                # itertools.product pairs each seq index with every token id
385	                logits_to_penalize.extend(
386	                    itertools.product(seqs_to_penalize, token_ids_to_penalize))
387	
388	    if logits_to_penalize:
389	        # use zip and * to group indices along each dimension
390	        # eg. [ (1,2), (1,3), (5,6) ] -> ( (1,1,5), (2,3,6) )
391	        logits[tuple(zip(*logits_to_penalize))] = -float("inf")
392	
393	    # verifies that no rows in logits were missed unexpectedly
394	    assert logits_applied == logits.shape[0]
395	    return logits

398	def _apply_top_k_top_p(
399	    logits: torch.Tensor,
400	    p: torch.Tensor,
401	    k: torch.Tensor,
402	) -> torch.Tensor:
403	    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
404	
405	    # Apply top-k.
406	    top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
407	    # Get all the top_k values.
408	    top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
409	    top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
410	    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
411	
412	    # Apply top-p.
413	    probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
414	    probs_sum = probs_sort.cumsum(dim=-1)
415	    top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
416	    # at least one
417	    top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
418	    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
419	
420	    # Re-sort the probabilities.
421	    logits = torch.empty_like(logits_sort).scatter_(dim=-1,
422	                                                    index=logits_idx,
423	                                                    src=logits_sort)
424	    return logits

434	    """
435	    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
436	    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
437	    scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
438	    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_min_p
439	    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
440	
441	    return logits

self,
        logits: torch.Tensor,
        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
    ):
        """The goal here is to reuse sampling tensors between similar decode
        runs. This is possible because sampling logic does not change between
        decodes of the same sequences.
        """
        _, vocab_size = logits.shape

        # First free any existing stored sampling tensors.
        # This is necessary because some sampling tensors may
        # have pinned memory.
        self._sampling_tensors = None

        # Initialize new sampling tensors
        (sampling_tensors, do_penalties, do_top_p_top_k,
         do_min_p) = SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata(
             sampling_metadata, vocab_size, logits.device, logits.dtype)

        self._sampling_tensors = sampling_tensors
        self._do_penalties = do_penalties
        self._do_top_p_top_k = do_top_p_top_k
        self._do_min_p = do_min_p

    def forward(
        self,
        logits: torch.Tensor,
        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
    ) -> Optional[SamplerOutput]:
        """
        Single-step scheduling:
            * Perform GPU-side sampling computation & compute
            GPU-side logprobs tensor
            * Pythonize sampling result & logprobs tensor

        Multi-step scheduling:
            * Perform GPU-side sampling computation & compute
            GPU-side logprobs tensor
            * Defer Pythonization of sampling result & logprobs
            tensor
            * Encapsulate arguments required for deferred Pythonization
            in the
            [`SamplerOutput`][vllm.model_executor.layers.sampler.SamplerOutput]
            structure

        Args:
            logits: (num_tokens, vocab_size).
            sampling_metadata: Metadata for sampling.
        """
        assert logits is not None
        _, vocab_size = logits.shape

        # Prepare sampling tensors with pinned memory to avoid blocking.
        if not sampling_metadata.reuse_sampling_tensors:
            self._init_sampling_tensors(logits, sampling_metadata)
        elif self._do_penalties:
            # In this case, the sampling tensors logic depends on
            # "output_tokens" of a sequence. As a result, we cannot
            # reuse sampling tensors, since "output_tokens" changes
            # between decode runs.
            self._init_sampling_tensors(logits, sampling_metadata)

        assert self._sampling_tensors is not None
        sampling_tensors = self._sampling_tensors
        do_penalties = self._do_penalties
        do_top_p_top_k = self._do_top_p_top_k
        do_min_p = self._do_min_p

        logits = _apply_min_tokens_penalty(logits, sampling_metadata)

        # Apply presence and frequency penalties.
        if do_penalties:
            logits = apply_penalties(logits, sampling_tensors.prompt_tokens,
                                     sampling_tensors.output_tokens,
                                     sampling_tensors.presence_penalties,
                                     sampling_tensors.frequency_penalties,
                                     sampling_tensors.repetition_penalties)

        # Use float32 to apply temperature scaling.
        # Use in-place division to avoid creating a new tensor.
        logits = logits.to(torch.float)
        logits.div_(sampling_tensors.temperatures.unsqueeze(dim=1))

        if do_top_p_top_k and flashinfer_top_k_top_p_sampling is None:
            logits = _apply_top_k_top_p(logits, sampling_tensors.top_ps,
                                        sampling_tensors.top_ks)

        if do_min_p:
            logits = _apply_min_p(logits, sampling_tensors.min_ps)

        # We use float32 for probabilities and log probabilities.
        # Compute the probabilities.
        probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1, dtype=torch.float)
        # Compute the log probabilities.
        logprobs = torch.log_softmax(logits, dim=-1, dtype=torch.float)

        # Sample the next tokens.
        maybe_deferred_sample_results, maybe_sampled_tokens_tensor = _sample(
            probs,
            logprobs,
            sampling_metadata,
            sampling_tensors,
            include_gpu_probs_tensor=self.include_gpu_probs_tensor,
            modify_greedy_probs=self._should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace,
        )

        if self.include_gpu_probs_tensor:
            # Since we will defer sampler result Pythonization,
            # preserve GPU-side tensors in support of later
            # deferred pythonization of logprobs
            assert maybe_sampled_tokens_tensor is not None
            on_device_tensors = (probs, logprobs, maybe_sampled_tokens_tensor)
        else:
            # Since Pythonization has already happened, don't preserve
            # GPU-side tensors.
            on_device_tensors = None

        # Get the logprobs query results.
        prompt_logprobs = None
        sample_logprobs = None
        if not sampling_metadata.skip_sampler_cpu_output:
            # Pythonize logprobs now (GPU -> CPU); do not defer.
            assert not isinstance(maybe_deferred_sample_results,
                                  SampleResultArgsType)
            prompt_logprobs, sample_logprobs = get_logprobs(
                logprobs, sampling_metadata, maybe_deferred_sample_results)

        return _build_sampler_output(
            maybe_deferred_sample_results,
            sampling_metadata,
            prompt_logprobs,
            sample_logprobs,
            on_device_tensors=on_device_tensors,
            skip_sampler_cpu_output=sampling_metadata.skip_sampler_cpu_output)

    @property
    def _should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace(self) -> bool:
        """Whether or not the sampler should modify the probability distribution
        of greedily-sampled tokens such that multinomial sampling would sample
        the greedily-sampled token.

        In other words, if True then we set the probability of the greedily-
        sampled token to 1.

        This is used by speculative decoding, which requires that the sampling
        method be encoded into the probability distribution.
        """
        return self.should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace


def _apply_min_tokens_penalty(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """Apply min_tokens penalty which sets stop tokens to -inf if min_tokens
        have not been generated yet
    """
    # list of indices in logits that will be set to -inf
    logits_to_penalize: list[tuple[int, int]] = []
    logits_applied = 0
    for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
        seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
        sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params

        sample_indices = seq_group.sample_indices
        logits_applied += len(sample_indices) + len(
            seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
        if not seq_group.do_sample:
            continue

        start_idx = sample_indices[0]
        min_tokens = sampling_params.min_tokens
        token_ids_to_penalize = sampling_params.all_stop_token_ids
        if min_tokens > 0 and token_ids_to_penalize:
            seqs_to_penalize: list[int] = []
            for j, seq_id in enumerate(seq_ids):
                seq_data = seq_group.seq_data[seq_id]
                if len(seq_data.output_token_ids_array) < min_tokens:
                    seqs_to_penalize.append(j)

            if seqs_to_penalize:
                # convert to the index into logits
                seqs_to_penalize = [start_idx + j for j in seqs_to_penalize]
                # itertools.product pairs each seq index with every token id
                logits_to_penalize.extend(
                    itertools.product(seqs_to_penalize, token_ids_to_penalize))

    if logits_to_penalize:
        # use zip and * to group indices along each dimension
        # eg. [ (1,2), (1,3), (5,6) ] -> ( (1,1,5), (2,3,6) )
        logits[tuple(zip(*logits_to_penalize))] = -float("inf")

    # verifies that no rows in logits were missed unexpectedly
    assert logits_applied == logits.shape[0]
    return logits


def _apply_top_k_top_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    p: torch.Tensor,
    k: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)

    # Apply top-k.
    top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
    # Get all the top_k values.
    top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
    top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))

    # Apply top-p.
    probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
    probs_sum = probs_sort.cumsum(dim=-1)
    top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
    # at least one
    top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))

    # Re-sort the probabilities.
    logits = torch.empty_like(logits_sort).scatter_(dim=-1,
                                                    index=logits_idx,
                                                    src=logits_sort)
    return logits


def _apply_min_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    min_p: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """
    Adapted from
    https://github.com/oobabooga/text-generation-webui/blob/3146124ec01f02c8fb1650a6517cf1b60b537aaf/modules/sampler_hijack.py#L16C17-L16C17
    """
    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
    scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_min_p
    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))

    return logits


def _greedy_sample(
    selected_seq_groups: list[SequenceGroupToSample],
    samples: torch.Tensor,
) -> SampleResultType:
    """Run greedy sampling on a given samples.

371	
372	@dataclass
373	class SamplingTensors:
374	    """Tensors for sampling."""
375	
376	    temperatures: torch.Tensor
377	    top_ps: torch.Tensor
378	    top_ks: torch.Tensor
379	    min_ps: torch.Tensor
380	    presence_penalties: torch.Tensor
381	    frequency_penalties: torch.Tensor
382	    repetition_penalties: torch.Tensor
383	    prompt_tokens: torch.Tensor
384	    output_tokens: torch.Tensor
385	
386	    @classmethod
387	    def from_sampling_metadata(
388	        cls,
389	        sampling_metadata: "SamplingMetadata",
390	        vocab_size: int,
391	        device: torch.device,
392	        dtype: torch.dtype,
393	    ) -> tuple["SamplingTensors", bool, bool, bool]:
394	        prompt_tokens: list[array] = []
395	        output_tokens: list[array] = []
396	        top_ks: list[int] = []
397	        temperatures: list[float] = []
398	        top_ps: list[float] = []
399	        min_ps: list[float] = []
400	        presence_penalties: list[float] = []
401	        frequency_penalties: list[float] = []
402	        repetition_penalties: list[float] = []
403	        do_penalties = False
404	        do_top_p_top_k = False
405	        do_min_p = False
406	
407	        assert sampling_metadata.seq_groups is not None
408	        for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
409	            seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
410	            sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
411	            temperature = sampling_params.temperature
412	            p = sampling_params.presence_penalty
413	            f = sampling_params.frequency_penalty
414	            r = sampling_params.repetition_penalty
415	            top_p = sampling_params.top_p
416	            min_p = sampling_params.min_p
417	
418	            # k should not be greater than the vocab size.
419	            top_k = min(sampling_params.top_k, vocab_size)
420	            top_k = vocab_size if top_k < 1 else top_k
421	            if temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
422	                # NOTE: Zero temperature means deterministic sampling
423	                # (i.e., greedy sampling or beam search).
424	                # Set the temperature to 1 to avoid division by zero.
425	                temperature = 1.0
426	            if not do_top_p_top_k and (top_p < 1.0 - _SAMPLING_EPS
427	                                       or top_k != vocab_size):
428	                do_top_p_top_k = True
429	            if not do_min_p and min_p > _SAMPLING_EPS:
430	                do_min_p = True
431	            if not do_penalties and (abs(p) >= _SAMPLING_EPS
432	                                     or abs(f) >= _SAMPLING_EPS
433	                                     or abs(r - 1.0) >= _SAMPLING_EPS):
434	                do_penalties = True
435	
436	            is_prompt = seq_group.is_prompt
437	            if is_prompt and sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None:
438	                # For tokens in the prompt that we only need to get
439	                # their logprobs
440	                query_len = seq_group.query_len
441	                assert query_len is not None
442	                prefill_len = len(seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
443	                temperatures += [temperature] * prefill_len
444	                top_ps += [top_p] * prefill_len
445	                top_ks += [top_k] * prefill_len
446	                min_ps += [min_p] * prefill_len
447	                presence_penalties += [0] * prefill_len
448	                frequency_penalties += [0] * prefill_len
449	                repetition_penalties += [1] * prefill_len
450	
451	            if seq_group.do_sample:
452	                sample_lens = len(seq_group.sample_indices)
453	                assert sample_lens >= len(seq_ids)
454	                temperatures += [temperature] * sample_lens
455	                top_ps += [top_p] * sample_lens
456	                top_ks += [top_k] * sample_lens
457	                min_ps += [min_p] * sample_lens
458	                presence_penalties += [p] * sample_lens
459	                frequency_penalties += [f] * sample_lens
460	                repetition_penalties += [r] * sample_lens
461	
462	        if do_penalties:
463	            for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
464	                seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
465	                sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
466	                if (seq_group.is_prompt
467	                        and sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None):
468	                    prefill_len = len(seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
469	                    prompt_tokens.extend(
470	                        array(VLLM_TOKEN_ID_ARRAY_TYPE)
471	                        for _ in range(prefill_len))
472	                    output_tokens.extend(
473	                        array(VLLM_TOKEN_ID_ARRAY_TYPE)
474	                        for _ in range(prefill_len))
475	                if seq_group.do_sample:
476	                    for seq_id in seq_ids:
477	                        seq_data = seq_group.seq_data[seq_id]
478	                        prompt_tokens.append(seq_data.prompt_token_ids_array)
479	                        output_tokens.append(seq_data.output_token_ids_array)
480	
481	        sampling_tensors = SamplingTensors.from_lists(
482	            temperatures,
483	            top_ps,
484	            top_ks,
485	            min_ps,
486	            presence_penalties,
487	            frequency_penalties,
488	            repetition_penalties,
489	            prompt_tokens,
490	            output_tokens,
491	            vocab_size,
492	            device,
493	            dtype,
494	        )
495	        return (sampling_tensors, do_penalties, do_top_p_top_k, do_min_p)
496	
497	    @classmethod
498	    def from_lists(
499	        cls,
500	        temperatures: list[float],
501	        top_ps: list[float],
502	        top_ks: list[int],
503	        min_ps: list[float],
504	        presence_penalties: list[float],
505	        frequency_penalties: list[float],
506	        repetition_penalties: list[float],
507	        prompt_tokens: list[array],
508	        output_tokens: list[array],
509	        vocab_size: int,
510	        device: torch.device,
511	        dtype: torch.dtype,
512	    ) -> "SamplingTensors":
513	        # Note that the performance will be very bad without
514	        # pinned memory.
515	        pin_memory = is_pin_memory_available()
516	
517	        do_penalties = prompt_tokens or output_tokens
518	
519	        if do_penalties:
520	            prompt_t = make_tensor_with_pad(
521	                prompt_tokens,
522	                vocab_size,
523	                device="cpu",
524	                dtype=torch.int64,
525	                pin_memory=pin_memory,
